blockingIssues.description: Relay runtime entrypoint (src/relay/index.ts:39) does not pass messageStore to createRelayServer. All messaging routes (/messages, /inbox, /messages/:id/read, /messages/:id/ack) are gated by `&& messageStore` checks in server.ts, so they are effectively disabled in the production relay startup path. Only test-only construction exercises these routes.

Relay dedupe lookup in src/relay/message-store.ts:176 is keyed only by sender_id + dedupe_key without validating recipient/in_reply_to/thread compatibility. Reusing the same key in an incompatible context (different recipient, different reply parent) cross-collides instead of rejecting, violating VAL-RELAY-009. Tests at test/relay/thread-dedupe.test.ts:418 encode this incorrect cross-colliding behavior.

Three gaps: (1) CLI send/inbox/read/ack paths (src/cli.ts) never use RelayClient — local message-store is still the only path, so CLI-observable retry/offline behavior is absent. (2) Offline queue (src/relay/client.ts:154) is in-memory only — queued messages are lost on process restart, violating local-first durability. (3) flush() does single-shot attempts without retry/backoff during reconciliation (src/relay/client.ts:228), leaving transient failures unrecovered.

Cipher runtime (encrypt/decrypt) was added and tested in isolation, but relay path still sends and handles plaintext body: src/relay/client.ts:286 sends `body: payload.body` in plaintext, and src/relay/server.ts:268 reads/stores/returns plaintext body. E2EE is not integrated into the relay transport path, violating VAL-E2EE-003 (ciphertext-only wire), VAL-E2EE-004 (recipient decrypt), and relay-path VAL-E2EE-009 (tamper detection on wire).

performKeyExchange() in src/e2ee/key-exchange.ts:186 never checks revocation state (isDeviceRevoked). After revokeDevice() marks a device revoked at line 475, callers can still perform a fresh key exchange with that device, restoring decrypt capability and violating the rotation/revocation trust boundary (VAL-E2EE-006). Test assertions at test/e2ee/rekey-rotation.test.ts:338 check CipherError on decrypt but not the key-exchange-blocked path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add implementation convention: 'Workers implementing relay-path features must verify that their code is wired into the relay production entrypoint (src/relay/index.ts), not only exercised in test-only server construction. Features that add optional dependencies to createRelayServer must also update the relay entrypoint to provide them.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: relay-async-send-inbox-read-ack-sync added messageStore-gated routes to server.ts but never updated index.ts to provide messageStore, leaving all messaging routes disabled in production. Same pattern risk exists for future optional relay dependencies.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add implementation convention: 'Relay dedupe-key scope must include context fields (recipient, thread, reply parent) — not just sender+key. Workers modifying dedupe behavior must verify against VAL-RELAY-009 incompatible-context non-collision requirement.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: relay-thread-reply-dedupe feature implemented sender+key-only dedupe in message-store.ts without context-compatibility validation, and tests encoded this incorrect behavior as expected.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add implementation convention: 'E2EE revocation must be enforced at key-exchange time, not just at decrypt time. performKeyExchange() must check isDeviceRevoked() and reject exchanges with revoked devices.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: e2ee-rekey-rotation feature stored revocation state but never enforced it during key exchange, allowing revoked devices to re-establish trust. Tests verified decrypt failure but not key-exchange prevention.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen mors-global-worker skill: require explicit evidence (or documented rationale) for manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks in worker handoffs. Multiple workers in this milestone marked followedProcedure=true without any manual verification evidence despite feature expectations requiring CLI-observable behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 4 of 6 features had shared-state observations noting that workers claimed followedProcedure=true without manual check evidence: relay-async-send-inbox-read-ack-sync, relay-thread-reply-dedupe, relay-offline-sync-and-transient-retry-recovery, relay-sender-binding-and-spoof-prevention.

rejectedObservations.observation: Shared guidance does not clearly encode relay dedupe-context compatibility rules
rejectedObservations.reason: Subsumed by the AGENTS.md suggestion about dedupe-key scope. The validation contract already specifies this (VAL-RELAY-009), so this is an enforcement/guidance gap rather than a missing library entry.
